entries.title: Denethor’s Stewardship Is Consumed By Despair

context.label: Denethor before his death

content.fact: Denethor remains proud and capable, but grief, pride, the war, and distorted knowledge drive him into despair and destructive command choices.
content.injection: When Denethor is active in Return of the King, portray him as proud, perceptive, grieving, and despairing rather than merely foolish or cowardly. His despair shapes Gondor’s crisis.
constraints: His love for Boromir and harshness toward Faramir should inform his behavior.
antiLore: Do not reduce Denethor to a one-note madman with no intelligence or political weight.

entries.title: Denethor Is Competent Before Despair Consumes Him

context.label: Denethor Is Competent Before Despair Consumes Him

content.fact: Denethor is proud, perceptive, politically experienced, and capable before despair drives catastrophe.
content.injection: Write Denethor as formidable and tragic, not merely foolish or mad from his first appearance.

context.label: Beregond And Bergil Ground Minas Tirith

content.fact: Beregond and Bergil give Pippin a grounded view of Minas Tirith through service, family, fear, and loyalty.
content.injection: Use Beregond and Bergil to humanize Gondor beyond kings, stewards, and armies.

entries.title: Eowyn Rides Hidden As Dernhelm

context.label: Eowyn before revealing herself on Pelennor

content.fact: Eowyn secretly rides to war disguised as Dernhelm, carrying Merry with her against orders and expectations.
content.injection: Before her reveal on Pelennor, do not have most characters know Dernhelm is Eowyn unless the story has explicitly exposed her disguise.
constraints: Merry is directly connected to this hidden riding state.
antiLore: Do not have the Rohirrim openly discuss Eowyn riding as herself before the reveal.

entries.title: Druedain Open The Hidden Road For Rohan

context.label: Druedain guidance before the Pelennor arrival

content.fact: Ghan-buri-Ghan and the Druedain guide Rohan by a hidden road, helping the Rohirrim avoid enemy watches and reach the Pelennor at the decisive time.
content.injection: For book-continuity Rohan’s ride, include the Druedain as crucial local guides rather than making the arrival a simple unassisted cavalry march.
constraints: The Druedain role is practical and local.
Theoden’s host still carries urgency and risk.
antiLore: Do not erase the Druedain guidance from book-canon Pelennor setup.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

entries.title: Theoden Rides Knowing The Cost

context.label: Theoden Rides Knowing The Cost

content.fact: Theoden’s ride to Gondor is a conscious kingly act under mortal risk, grief, and renewed purpose.
content.injection: Theoden at Pelennor should feel aged, resolute, and sacrificial rather than invincible.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Faramir’s Wounding Creates A Stewardship Crisis

context.label: Faramir wounded through healing

content.fact: Faramir is gravely wounded during Gondor’s defense, making Denethor’s despair and Gondor’s leadership crisis acute.
content.injection: After Faramir is wounded, treat his condition as a major emotional and political pressure point for Minas Tirith, Denethor, and the Stewardship.
constraints: Faramir is not dead during the pyre crisis.
antiLore: Do not treat Faramir as healthy and active in command during his wound-and-healing window.

entries.title: Beregond Risks Treason For Faramir

context.label: Beregond’s treason risk before Faramir is healed

content.fact: Beregond breaks strict guard duty and risks treason to help save Faramir from Denethor’s pyre.
content.injection: During the pyre crisis, include Beregond as a loyal Gondorian whose disobedience is morally necessary but politically dangerous.
constraints: The act should carry real legal and personal risk.
Pippin’s alarm and Beregond’s intervention both matter.
antiLore: Do not make Faramir’s rescue only Gandalf arriving at the last second with no local Gondorian agency.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

entries.title: Theoden Falls At Pelennor

context.label: Theoden’s fall and immediate aftermath

content.fact: Theoden leads Rohan’s charge at Pelennor and dies after being crushed by Snowmane during the battle with the Witch-king.
content.injection: After Theoden’s fall, Rohan scenes should reflect grief, sacrifice, and the transition of leadership rather than treating him as still commanding.
constraints: His death occurs within the Pelennor battle sequence.
antiLore: Do not keep Theoden alive and politically active after Pelennor in book continuity unless AU.

context.label: Aragorn Delays Public Kingship Until The Right Moment

content.fact: Aragorn exercises command and healing before coronation but delays formal entry as king.
content.injection: Keep Aragorn restrained after Pelennor; legitimacy grows before formal coronation.

entries.title: Aragorn’s Healing Signals Kingship

context.label: Houses of Healing through coronation

content.fact: Aragorn’s healing of Faramir, Eowyn, and Merry fulfills the sign that the hands of the king are the hands of a healer.
content.injection: After the Houses of Healing, Aragorn’s kingship is strengthened by healing and recognition, not just battlefield command or bloodline.
constraints: This is an important legitimacy step before coronation.
antiLore: Do not make Aragorn’s kingship purely military or purely hereditary when the healing sign is relevant.

coordinates.label: Book VI: The End of the Third Age

entries.title: Sam Carries Frodo Through Mordor’s End

context.label: Frodo and Sam in final Mordor approach

content.fact: Near the end of the Mordor road, Sam’s loyalty becomes physically and morally decisive as Frodo is nearly spent by the Ring and the journey.
content.injection: In final Mordor scenes, Sam should be exhausted but steadfast, helping Frodo continue when Frodo can barely carry himself or the Ring’s burden.
constraints: Sam cannot simply remove the burden from Frodo, but he can carry Frodo and support him.
antiLore: Do not make Frodo breezily strong or Sam detached during the final Mordor approach.

entries.title: Frodo Claims The Ring At The Crack Of Doom

context.label: Crack of Doom and Ring destruction

content.fact: At the Crack of Doom, Frodo does not willingly destroy the Ring; he claims it, and the Ring is destroyed only through Gollum’s intervention and fall.
content.injection: At the Crack of Doom, Frodo does not complete the quest by clean willpower. He claims the Ring; destruction occurs through Gollum’s intervention and fall.
constraints: This should be gated to Mount Doom only.
The moment should preserve pity, failure, providence, and accumulated Ring pressure.
antiLore: Do not portray Frodo as voluntarily throwing the Ring into the fire in book continuity.

entries.title: Frodo Claims The Ring At Sammath Naur

context.label: Frodo Claims The Ring At Sammath Naur

content.fact: At the Crack of Doom, Frodo cannot willingly destroy the Ring and instead claims it.
content.injection: At Mount Doom, Frodo’s failure is essential canon; do not make destruction a clean voluntary heroic act.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.

entries.title: Arwen Arrival Completes Kingship Public Resolution

context.label: Arwen arrives after Aragorn’s coronation

content.fact: Arwen’s arrival in Minas Tirith belongs to the late public restoration of Aragorn’s kingship, not to the earlier war-road scenes.
content.injection: After coronation, let Aragorn and Arwen’s union complete the restoration context. Before this window, keep Arwen’s role distant and thematically weighty rather than constantly present in the war front.
constraints: This is a late-resolution relationship state.
It should not overwrite Aragorn’s earlier hidden-claim arc.
antiLore: Do not insert Arwen as a routine battlefield companion in this book-continuity deck.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.
